_note: Two-plane decomposition for TKT-0011 / ADR-0123 — the new ui-color-picker edit-class control (+ ui-text-field type=color leg). OUTSIDE-IN nodes = the surfaces the control + its composition + its migration touch (traits/, controls/color-picker/, controls/text-field/, catalog, site, gates). INSIDE-OUT actions = what the control must DO. Crossed via hosts. plan:true — each leaf carries the checkable acceptance predicate the builder's tests satisfy; no source exists yet under controls/color-picker/ or traits/area-drag.ts. Node ids align to color-picker.spec.md §5 Trace. Waves: M1 = the standalone control (n3..n18,n26 + gates); M1b = the type=color leg (n21,n22); M2 = the catalog row (n20). The 2D pad is the novelty leg (n14 area-drag trait + n15 pad a11y/keyboard); the accessible spine is the channel sliders (n10), NOT the pad.

nodes.label: ui-color-picker + type=color leg (the whole intake)
the standalone ui-color-picker control
tag ui-color-picker, class UIColorPickerElement, folder controls/color-picker/, extends UIFormElement, idempotent self-define + idempotent #ensureShell
nodes.accept: `npm test color-picker` — jsdom: `customElements.get('ui-color-picker')` resolves to a UIFormElement subclass with formAssociated true; connect→disconnect→reconnect creates each [data-part] exactly once (no duplicate pad/readout)

nodes.label: props schema (value / format)
value prop — reflected string, default '' (the serialized, form-facing surface)
nodes.accept: `npm test color-picker` — jsdom: fresh instance `el.value === ''`; `el.value='#3b82f6'` reflects and repositions thumb+sliders; unset submits nothing

nodes.label: format prop — reflected enum ['hex','oklch'], default 'hex'; selects value serialization only, model stays OKLCH
nodes.accept: `npm test color-picker` — jsdom: default 'hex'; `el.format='oklch'` reflects and re-serializes `value` to `oklch(L C H)`; an out-of-vocab format value falls back to 'hex' (fail-open, no crash)

nodes.label: color.ts numerics — OKLCH↔sRGB + gamutMapChroma binary search (pure, zero-dep, promoted from adia never ported)
nodes.accept: `npm test color` — unit: `hexToOklch∘oklchToHex` round-trips within ε; `gamutMapChroma` returns an in-gamut chroma for a wide-gamut triple and is a no-op in-gamut; no DOM import (color.ts is import-free)

nodes.label: colorCodecOptions(format) — the ADR-0044/0047-dialect codec {parse,format,errorMessage}, pure, reused by the type=color leg
nodes.accept: `npm test color` — unit: `colorCodecOptions('hex')` parses #rgb/#rrggbb/oklch(...) → #rrggbb; `('oklch')` formats to oklch(L C H) at authored chroma; unparseable → null; errorMessage present

nodes.label: anatomy — idempotent light-DOM parts pad/pad-thumb/channels/readout built once (the ui-calendar shell precedent)
nodes.accept: `npm test color-picker` — jsdom: [data-part=pad], [data-part=pad-thumb], [data-part=channels], [data-part=readout] all present after connect; a canvas lives inside the pad

nodes.label: channel sliders — composed ui-slider (hue always; chroma+lightness mirrored) = the ACCESSIBLE SPINE (SPEC-R6), each an audited UIRangeElement role=slider
nodes.accept: `npm test color-picker` + `npm run test:browser color-picker` — every channel is a real <ui-slider>; keyboard-only Tab reaches each and arrow keys change its value (input/change per the slider contract); a channel's aria-valuetext announces its value (read via internals pattern)

nodes.label: readout — embedded editable ui-text-field two-way-bound to the serialized value through colorCodecOptions (the 'precision input' ADR-0118 named as owed)
nodes.accept: `npm test color-picker` — typing #3b82f6 or oklch(...) into the readout and committing (blur/Enter) parses through colorCodecOptions and updates the model + fires change; an invalid entry surfaces customError, no change

nodes.label: preview + presets — composed ui-swatch preview (NOT a bespoke color div — the ADR-0118 fence) + optional [slot=presets]
nodes.accept: `npm test color-picker` — the preview is a real <ui-swatch> whose value tracks the color (grep: no background-painting div outside the pad); [slot=presets] renders author content, and its absence leaves no layout hole

nodes.label: events — input (live: drag/step) + change (commit: pointer-up/channel-blur-with-move/readout-parse-success); both ⊂ allowlist, no new name
nodes.accept: `npm run test:browser color-picker` — a pad drag fires repeated input + exactly one change on pointer-up; a drag returning to start fires no change (the ui-slider precedent)

nodes.label: area-drag trait — NEW 2-axis pointer→(x,y) gesture (sibling of value-drag.ts; value-drag reused unchanged by the channels)
nodes.accept: `npm test area-drag` (unit, INSTRUMENT-BRIDGE) — synthetic pointerdown+move on a stubbed area with mocked getBoundingClientRect maps onValue(x,y) both axes clamped [0,1]; the per-drag AbortController leaves zero move-listener residue after pointer-up (the value-drag.test.ts precedent)

nodes.label: pad a11y + 2-axis keyboard — [data-part=pad] tabindex=0, role=slider + aria-roledescription='2D slider' + cross-announcing aria-valuetext (on the PART, ui-calendar precedent); bespoke ←/→ chroma, ↑/↓ lightness, Shift coarse, Home/End/PageUp/Down, preventDefault all
nodes.accept: `npm test color-picker` (jsdom: role/roledescription/aria-valuetext read off the part; arrow keys change the right channel) + `npm run test:browser color-picker` (real arrow keys move the expected axis and update aria-valuetext)

nodes.label: whole-shape + non-color signifier (ADR-0102/0057) — bare picker paints a non-collapsed operable control with zero CSS; every channel prints a numeric value
nodes.accept: `npm run test:browser color-picker` — a bare <ui-color-picker> in a realistic container has a non-degenerate bounding box (pad real height via --ui-color-picker-pad-block-size, channels real width — the ui-slider DOT-bug guard); a numeric readout of each channel is present as text

nodes.label: form seams — formValue/formValidity(valueMissing+customError)/formReset/formStateRestore + applyFieldLabelling + trackUserInvalid (the calendar/text-field precedent)
nodes.accept: `npm test color-picker` — in a <form>: submission includes name=value when set, omits when unset; required+unset ⇒ valueMissing; unparseable readout ⇒ customError; formReset restores initial value AND clears :state(user-invalid)

nodes.label: descriptor color-picker.md (tier: pattern, extends UIFormElement, formAssociated true) + the contract↔props/source trip-wires
nodes.accept: `npm test` — validateComponentDescriptor + compareDescriptorToProps + compareDescriptorToSource pass with zero drift; attributes[] mirror value/format/formProps 1:1; parts/slots/events/customStates/forcedColors declared

nodes.label: forced-colors — explicit @media(forced-colors:active): pad degrades to Canvas + CanvasText-bordered thumb (never a fake color); channels+readout stay the operable path
nodes.accept: `npm run test:browser color-picker` (forced-colors emulated) — the channel sliders + numeric readout remain operable and legible; the pad shows no fabricated system color

nodes.label: EyeDropper — feature-detected ('EyeDropper' in window) progressive enhancement (SPEC-R13/F7): the affordance appears iff supported, no polyfill, no layout hole when absent; a sample commits through the model
nodes.accept: `npm run test:browser color-picker` — WebKit/Firefox: no eyedropper button and no layout hole (absent branch); Chromium with a stubbed window.EyeDropper resolving a color: the button is present, activation invokes the API, and a resolved sample commits + fires change (present branch — the API stubbed, the OS picker not driven)

nodes.label: catalog disposition (M2 wave)

catalog — M1 seeds EXCLUSION_ALLOWLIST('ColorPicker'); M2 lands the ColorPicker row (value:{prop:'value',event:'change'}, ADR-0019) + exemplar + §5.2 guidance + FEED_EXCLUDED, allowlist drained to zero residue
nodes.accept: `npm test` (a2ui catalog/default/index.test.ts) — at M1 ColorPicker enters FLEET_TYPES and the coverage gate is green ONLY via the allowlist seed (residue-guard proves it is not also a catalog key); at M2 the row + exemplar validate 0-CATALOG-error, allowlist residue is zero, FEED_EXCLUDED carries the entry

nodes.label: the ui-text-field type=color leg (M1b — the ADR-0048 lazy-overlay seam)
text-field type=color config row + NEW affordance 'swatch' + codec 'color' wiring colorCodecOptions through the valueCodec trait
nodes.accept: `npm test text-field` — a type=color field carries a trailing swatch affordance showing the current color via a composed ui-swatch; typed entry parses through colorCodecOptions(format); the twelve prior types are unaffected

nodes.label: lazy overlay ensureColorPicker — first-activation dynamic import('../color-picker/color-picker.ts') + <ui-color-picker> in a Wave-4 overlay popup; picker change forwarded to field value; tree-shake holds
nodes.accept: `npm run test:browser text-field` + tree-shake test — a type=text/number field's STATIC graph excludes color-picker.ts; a type=color field carries no picker subtree until first swatch-button activation, then mounts the picker in the overlay and a pick commits back to the field value; the Enter-preventDefault anchor-refocus guard holds (ADR-0045/0048)

nodes.label: site pages (tier: pattern requires {doc, demo})
color-picker-doc.ts — the descriptor-derived API doc page
nodes.accept: `npm test` (site-coverage.test.ts) — color-picker-doc exists, derived from color-picker.md (attributes/parts/slots/events/geometry), and the required-page-set check for a tier:pattern control passes

nodes.label: color-picker-demo.ts — a composition demo (a themed color field + a standalone picker with preset swatches in the slot; and the type=color field leg)
nodes.accept: `npm test` (site-coverage.test.ts) — color-picker-demo exists, satisfying the pattern tier's {doc,demo} required set, showing both vehicles (standalone + type=color) and the presets slot

nodes.label: gates
unit tests — color.test.ts (numerics+codec) + area-drag.test.ts (trait), pure/isolated
nodes.accept: `npm test color area-drag` green — the numerics, gamut binary search, codec parse/format, and the 2-axis gesture mapping + AbortController lifetime all pass without the full element

nodes.label: jsdom probes — model round-trip, format switch, form seams, pad ARIA-via-part-read, keyboard axis routing, ui-swatch-not-a-div
nodes.accept: `npm test color-picker` green, including a negative control that an unparseable value leaves the prior model intact (proving the guard bites, not just the happy path)

nodes.label: cross-engine browser probes — whole-shape + REAL pointer-drag on pad (2-axis) AND channels + 2-axis real-key proof + forced-colors (Chromium+WebKit)
nodes.accept: `npm run test:browser color-picker` — the pad thumb tracks a real pointer in BOTH axes and the emitted color changes in chroma+lightness (the area-drag proof a synthetic dispatch can't give); canvas PIXELS are not asserted (paint is not the contract), thumb position + emitted value are (the whole-shape law)

nodes.label: barrels/exports/size integration (ADR-0080 marginal pin; ADR-0040 manual size discipline)
nodes.accept: `npm run check && npm test` green (barrels.test.ts, exports drift, tree-shake.test.ts); `npm run size` run by hand — this composite (pad+canvas+codec+math) likely re-bases the family total; the marginal is measured and pinned

nodes.label: independent component-reviewer GO before the wave commit
nodes.accept: a fresh-context component-reviewer pass returns GO against the descriptor + anatomy + geometry + contract fidelity + the whole-shape law (the standing per-control-wave DoD)

actions.label: identify itself under the fleet ui-* tag law as a form-associated composite, building its parts exactly once
hold the current color as a serialized, form-facing value that defaults to unset
let the author choose whether that value speaks hex or oklch, while working internally in OKLCH
convert between OKLCH and sRGB and gamut-map an out-of-gamut color back into the monitor, with no library
parse any accepted color syntax into the model and format the model back out, as reusable pure functions
lay out a pad, channel rows, and a readout as stable light-DOM parts
let a keyboard or screen-reader user set every channel through a real slider, never needing the pad
accept precise typed color entry and reflect it back, editable
preview the current color by composing the display sibling, never re-owning color rendering, and accept author-supplied presets
stream input while a gesture is live and commit a single change when it ends
map a two-axis pointer gesture on the pad to two channel values
present the pad as an accessible 2D-slider accelerator with two-axis keyboard control and cross-announced values
paint a visible, non-collapsed, operable control with zero consumer CSS and never signify state by hue alone
participate in a form — submit, validate, reset, restore, label, and time its own invalid state
tell the truth about its own attributes, parts, and states in its descriptor
degrade honestly under forced colors, keeping the channel and readout input path operable
offer an eyedropper only where the platform supports it, and never break where it doesn't
become an agent-emittable catalog type with a two-way value seam, out of the artifact feed
give ui-text-field a thirteenth type=color that shows the color and takes typed entry
open the full picker in an overlay only when first asked, without shipping its bytes to fields that don't use it
give the fleet a discoverable API doc page
give the fleet a composition demo covering both vehicles and the presets slot
prove its numerics, codec, and two-axis gesture in isolation
prove the model, form seams, ARIA, and keyboard under jsdom, including a biting negative control
prove the real pointer-drag and keyboard paint the right color, both engines
stay within the family size budget, exported through the public barrel
pass independent review before shipping

edges.why: the standalone control is the primary surface of the intake
the catalog disposition is a surface of the intake
the type=color leg is a surface of the intake
the site pages are a surface of the intake
the gates are a surface of the intake
the control's parts hang off its class + shell
the props schema is declared on the class
value is a member of the props schema
format is a member of the props schema
the numerics live in the control's color.ts
the codec lives in the control's color.ts
the anatomy is built by the control's shell
the channels are parts of the control
the readout is a part of the control
the preview/presets are parts of the control
the events are emitted by the control
the pad wires the area-drag trait
the pad a11y/keyboard is the control's
the whole-shape guarantee is the control's
the form seams are the control's
the descriptor describes the control
the forced-colors behavior is the control's
the eyedropper affordance is the control's
the catalog wave is the disposition's realization
the type=color config is the leg's surface
the lazy overlay is the leg's surface
the doc page is a site surface
the demo page is a site surface
the unit tests are a gate
the jsdom probes are a gate
the browser probes are a gate
the size/exports integration is a gate
the independent review is a gate
the codec is built on the numerics — parse/format call oklchToHex/hexToOklch
the codec is what serializes/parses the value surface
the codec's format branch is selected by the format prop
the channels are placed by the shell
the readout is placed by the shell
the preview is placed by the shell
the pad keyboard/a11y sits over the pad's pointer gesture
the readout two-way-binds through the codec
the channels are the accessible spine the pad accelerates
the swatch preview shows the gamut-mapped hex the numerics produce
the type=color leg reuses colorCodecOptions
the overlay mounts the standalone control, so it must exist first
the allowlist/catalog key derives from the descriptor's PascalCase type
the doc page derives from the descriptor
review runs once the gates it grades against are green
